title: What you must know before Mythos arrives | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
summary: Damien Lewke argues that AI has drastically reduced the barrier to launching cyberattacks, enabling small teams with just a GPU to execute sophisticated campaigns that previously required elite teams and years of expertise. He outlines the cyber kill chain stages, identifies three behavioral signals that reveal active attackers inside networks, and advocates for an "assume breach" defensive posture as the only realistic security model.
writeup: Damien Lewke of Nebulock argues that AI has lowered the barrier to launching cyberattacks, letting small teams with a single GPU run sophisticated campaigns that once demanded elite operators and years of expertise. He walks through the stages of the cyber kill chain and names three behavioral signals that expose attackers already operating inside a network. The central claim: an "assume breach" posture is the only realistic defensive model, shifting focus toward threat hunting and detecting active intruders rather than preventing every intrusion.
